title: Ollama EmbeddingProvider adapter (production impl)
description:
No production EmbeddingProvider adapter exists in internal/infrastructure/. Required for M3 gates 1 (embedder throughput), 2 (semantic recall), 3 (auto-link FP rate against real fixture). Spec:

- Package: internal/infrastructure/embedding/ollama/ollama.go.
- Implements ports.EmbeddingProvider: Embed(ctx, text) ([]float32, error) + ModelID() string.
- HTTP client against $VESKA_OLLAMA_URL (default http://localhost:11434); model name from $VESKA_EMBED_MODEL (default nomic-embed-text).
- Endpoint: POST /api/embeddings {model, prompt}; response {embedding: []float}.
- Error wrapping: any net.OpError / DNS failure / EOF / 5xx / connection refused MUST be wrapped with errors.Is-friendly fmt.Errorf containing ports.ErrEmbedderUnreachable. This is what triggers search.Service's lexical fallback (m3.03.2). 4xx body validation errors do NOT wrap the sentinel (those are caller faults; let them propagate).
- Timeout: configurable (Option), default 30s. Honour ctx cancellation.
- ModelID returns the configured model string verbatim. If empty, panic at construction.
- Functional options: WithBaseURL, WithModel, WithHTTPClient, WithTimeout.
- Tests: httptest.NewServer covering happy path, 5xx, connection refused (force-close listener), ctx cancel, wrong response shape.
- Dimension assertion: m3.02.1's worker passes dim to MarkReady; verify the adapter returns a stable dim for the configured model (don't enforce a hardcoded 768; the worker accepts whatever the adapter produces).

Blocked-by: nothing (independent piece). Blocks: solov2-3lv (daemon wiring needs to construct this), and via that, M3 gates 1/2/3.

title: m3.05 perf: revalidation sweep exceeds 60s for 10k findings (fsync-per-UPDATE bottleneck)
description:
Bench tools/loadtest/revalidate (added in solov2-64k.5.4) measures 69.8s wall time for the 10k-node / 10k-edge / 3k-stale-finding M3 exit-gate fixture. Target is <60s.

Profile: per-file Handle calls a sequence of single-row UPDATE statements via RevalidateRepo.RefreshAnchorHash (and CloseAsRevalidatedObsolete). With 3000 stale findings and default SQLite WAL+sync settings each UPDATE auto-commits and fsyncs, giving the observed ~23ms per finding * 3000 = ~70s.

Likely fix candidates:
  1. Batch all per-file refresh+close UPDATEs into a single tx inside Handler.Handle (or at the repo layer via a new BatchApply port method). This drops the fsync count from O(findings) to O(files) = 100, well inside budget.
  2. PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL on the eval bench DB — production may already do this; verify with sqlite.OpenWithOptions defaults.
  3. Combined UPDATE...WHERE finding_id IN (...) batches.

Smaller isolation sub-tests (4 files / 80 findings) pass comfortably (~1.5s wall) so the bottleneck is fsync count, not query plan.

Bench reproduction:
  make eval-revalidate-bench
Current run: elapsed=69815ms, p95_handle=1306ms, refreshed=3000, closed=0, gate=FAIL.

Blocks: solov2-64k.5.4 (revalidation timing bench DoD).
close_reason: DoD verified: ApplyDecisions port + adapter batches per-file UPDATEs in one tx (sql.Tx + prepared stmts); Handler.Handle uses one tx per file; metrics increment only post-commit; empty batch is no-op; pre-existing 5.2/5.3 tests green. Bench Combined10k: 69.8s → 6.4s (10.9× headroom under 60s gate). Commit 15e5e4c.

title: Compose daemon main loop (M3 wiring)
description:
cmd/veska-daemon/main.go is a 75-line shell. None of the long-running pieces are wired up:

- MCP Unix socket server (cli.sock + mcp.sock) — registry.MustRegister + RegisterTool* calls
- StagingArea + IngestionGate + Ingester (with SetFindingStorage(sqlite.FindingRepo))
- Promoter (with SetCheckRunner(checks.Runner registered with DeadCodeCheck + ContractDriftCheck))
- embedder.Worker (Start/Stop; default rate 10/s; max attempts 3)
- queue.Poller registering ports.WorkHandler for ports.WorkKindEmbed (Embedder is currently invoked direct; wire via poller for symmetry), AutoLink (autolink.Handler), Revalidate (revalidate.Handler)
- fsnotify watcher → Ingester.Save
- procmgr for any external supervised processes (sqlite-vec native? Ollama is external, no procmgr needed)
- Ollama EmbeddingProvider adapter (does not yet exist; missing) — must wrap network/connection errors with ports.ErrEmbedderUnreachable so search.Service's lexical fallback actually triggers.
- DI validation: every NewX constructor that panics on nil collaborator should be replaced by typed errors during composition root — verify with one failing test per missing dep.
- Schedule.Embedder workers, revalidate workers, autolink workers — start each as part of daemon Start; Stop on signal.
- Wiring depends on: m3.01 check pipeline, m3.02 embedder worker, m3.04 autolink handler, m3.05 revalidate handler. All five features now closed; this is the integration step.

title: m3.02.1 — Batch loop: pull pending, batch by 32, embed
description:
Goroutine that pulls node_embedding_refs where state='pending' and embeds in batches of 32. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.02.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
1. Enqueue path: this task wires Promoter to INSERT a pending row into node_embedding_refs for every promoted node (one extra prepared stmt in the existing tx, mirroring queueStmt).
2. Each batch: read text for the node, call EmbeddingProvider.Embed, write bytes into node_embeddings (content-addressed PK content_hash), set node_embedding_refs.state='ready' + content_hash + embedded_at, AND upsert into VectorStorage so m3.03 search can hit them in the same tick.
3. Lifecycle: long-running goroutine with Start(ctx)/Stop(); default poll cadence 250ms (matches post-promotion-queue worker pattern). NOT routed through post_promotion_queue.
4. Out of scope: rate limit (2.2), retry policy (2.3), content_hash dedup before calling Ollama (2.4). m3.02.1 calls Embed unconditionally per row.
close_reason: DoD verified: Promoter enqueues pending refs atomically; embedder.Worker drains in batches of ≤32, embeds, writes content-addressed node_embeddings, upserts VectorStorage, marks ref ready; Start/Stop race-clean (sync.Once + mu-guarded cancel); veska_embed_queue_depth gauge; per-row errors isolated; idempotent via ON CONFLICT. Commit 7f374c8. Full suite -race clean.

title: m3.01.1 — Synchronous check pipeline runner

Runner that executes registered structural checks after the promotion tx commits, before Promoter.Promote returns to the hook. See docs/milestones/M3.md §m3.01.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):
- Execution: post-tx (tx commits first, then checks run against the committed state). Findings are advisory and CANNOT abort a promotion. This task ships the dispatch shape; per-check rollback semantics are explicitly NOT supported.
- Findings persistence: bundle a FindingStorage port (internal/core/ports) + sqlite repo adapter (internal/infrastructure/sqlite) in this task so that m3.01.{2,3,4} only have to author check logic.
- Scope: interface (Check), registry, invocation site in Promoter, FindingStorage port + adapter, per-check timing emitted via the existing veska_seal_latency_seconds histogram (label per check name).
- Out of scope: actual check implementations (1.2/1.3/1.4).

title: M0 — Substrate Spike (sqlite-vec + branch-in-PK)
description:
# M0 — Substrate Spike

Canonical doc: `docs/milestones/M0.md` (extract below).

## Problem
ADR-S0001 names sqlite-vec as the entire vector substrate, and SOLO-08 §4 puts `branch` in the PK of nodes/edges/findings. Both rest on measurements that have not been taken. Building M1 on a failing assumption forces a fallback design under schedule pressure with code already written on top.

## Approach
Two throwaway Go spike harnesses against the target schemas — one for sqlite-vec at 50k/1M vectors, one for branch-in-PK at 50 branches × 100k symbols. Numbers feed decision matrices in M0.md §Outcomes. Failures amend the relevant ADR/schema before M1 starts.

## Goals
- Vector-substrate verdict (green/yellow/red-quality/red-ceiling) with warm p95, recall@10, vec0 ceiling at 50k and 1M
- Branch-in-PK verdict with row growth, disk size, indexed-lookup p95, GC sweep cost
- ADR-S0001, SOLO-08, SOLO-13, OQ-S001/S003/S006 updated against measured numbers
- M1 plan confirmed or amended before m1.03 begins

## Out of scope
- Any production code in `internal/`
- Parser, promotion pipeline, application layer
- Real-repo data (m1.08 re-verifies later)
- All spike code is throwaway; deleted or moved to attic when M0 closes

## Constraints
- Reference hardware: 8-core, 16 GiB RAM, NVMe SSD (M1 MacBook Air or equivalent x86-64 laptop). Numbers measured on this tier are normative.
- Vec0 ceiling must be ≥ 250 000 embedded nodes to avoid red-ceiling verdict.

## Risks / open questions
- OQ-S001 (vec0 latency/recall envelope), OQ-S003 (HNSW pivot trigger), OQ-S006 (branch-in-PK scaling) all resolve out of this milestone.
- A red verdict on either spike blocks M1's m1.03; m0.03 owns the amendment work.

See `docs/milestones/M0.md` for the full outcome tables, sub-issue checklist, and rationale.
close_reason: M0 complete: vec0 RED-CEILING (100k nodes, spike 4d63d34), branch-in-PK GREEN (0.039ms/0.047ms, spike 72d6ca4); ADR-S0001 amended, ADR-S0014 accepted, OQ-S001/S003/S006 resolved, M1 amended with m1.hnsw-pivot gate

description:
Once daemon main loop is wired (solov2-3lv), build a fuzz test that:
- Drives N synthetic promotions (default 100, env override) into the StagingArea + Promoter.

title: Choose trigram FTS5 + camelCase tokenizer + RRF (not plain BM25) for eng_search_semantic lexical fallback
description: The embedder-offline / embedding-pending fallback for eng_search_semantic is specified as plain BM25 over FTS5 on symbol names and tags (PRODUCT.md:45, docs/design/09-mcp-surface/README.md:388,442; SOLO-08 §3.3). Verified on 2026-05-14: this path is NOT yet implemented — no FTS5 table in migrations 0001-0004, no eng_search_semantic handler registered, no BM25 code in tree. The fallback is a forward-looking commitment scheduled for M3. Decision to make BEFORE M3 implementation begins: pick the right lexical backend up-front rather than ship plain BM25 and revisit. Plain BM25 underperforms on this corpus: symbol names are 1-4 tokens so TF collapses to 0/1, the default tokenizer doesn't split camelCase/snake_case, and tags are categorical. Proposed design, all native SQLite, no new deps: (1) FTS5 with the trigram tokenizer (built into SQLite ≥3.34) for substring + small-typo tolerance; (2) custom tokenizer that splits camelCase / snake_case / :: / . so closeFinding indexes as closeFinding, close, and Finding; (3) reciprocal rank fusion of BM25-on-words and BM25-on-trigrams: score = 1/(60+rank_words) + 1/(60+rank_trigram); (4) FTS5 prefix queries (foo*) for starts-with hits. Contract for eng_search_semantic and the degraded_reasons:['embedder_offline_lexical_fallback'] label are unchanged from the spec.
design: Single FTS5 virtual table with trigram tokenizer + tokenchars override for camelCase/snake_case splitting, OR two FTS5 tables (words + trigrams) fused at query time via RRF. Two-table RRF is cleaner to A/B against current BM25 and lets you keep BM25 ranking on each leg independently. Custom tokenizer is ~20 LOC against the FTS5 tokenizer API.
acceptance_criteria: (1) ADR amendment to SOLO-08 §3.3 documenting the switch from plain BM25 to trigram+RRF before the M3 lexical-fallback work begins. (2) When the M3 implementation lands, fallback returns hits for camelCase-fragment queries that plain BM25 would miss. (3) M3 measurement task compares recall@10 of the chosen design against plain BM25 on a representative symbol-search benchmark; chosen design wins by a measurable margin. (4) p95 latency stays within the SOLO-13 lexical-fallback budget.
notes: Surfaced during post-M1/post-M2 codebase review on 2026-05-14. Re-checked: not implemented yet — purely a design decision to land in the M3 ADR amendment, not a refactor.
close_reason: Superseded by solov2-64k.3.2 (trigram+RRF lexical fallback implementation, absorbs design) and solov2-64k.3.3 (bake-off vs plain BM25, absorbs AC3). M3 eval-harness will measure both designs on the same fixture per user request.

title: m3.05.3 — Supersede: chain old → new finding via closed_reason
description:
Refine 5.2's revalidate sweep: distinguish 'revalidated_obsolete' (rule no longer fires) from 'rule still fires' via per-rule re-run.

CLARIFIED 2026-05-14 (user 'proceed'):

Per-rule dispatch (NOT through the m3.01 Runner — wasteful):
- rule='dead-code' → re-run DeadCodeQuerier check against the new node state.
- rule='contract-drift' → re-run ContractDriftQuerier check.
- rule='auto-link' → always close as 'revalidated_obsolete' (heavy vector-search per finding deferred to a future bead).
- other rules (parse-failure has NULL anchor_content_hash, never enters this path) → conservative default: close as 'revalidated_obsolete'.

For dead-code/contract-drift IF the rule still fires on new content:
- REFRESH the existing finding's anchor_content_hash to the current node content_hash.
- Leave state='open'.
- NO new finding row, NO supersede link, NO closed_reason.
- This is a deliberate simplification from SOLO-04 §8.1's 'superseded_by_revalidation' ceremony — branch-stable hashed finding_id means 'still firing' = 'finding still relevant', so the supersede chain is unnecessary churn.

If the rule no longer fires:
- Close the finding with closed_reason='revalidated_obsolete' (5.2 behaviour preserved).

DROP the proposed superseded_by_finding_id migration (YAGNI — nothing references it after the simplification).

Doc update: amend SOLO-04 §8.1 + M3.md §m3.05 in the same commit to reflect the simplified semantics (state ∈ {open, closed}; closed_reason ∈ {revalidated_obsolete, accept, ...}; 'superseded_by_revalidation' is documented as considered-and-dropped).

This commit RE-WRITES m3.05.2's handler — turns the blunt 'close everything' into the per-rule dispatch above.

title: Dual VectorStorage backend: sqlite-vec (default) + usearch (scale)
description:
Add a sqlite-vec VectorStorage adapter alongside the existing usearch adapter, controlled by a config flag (vector.backend: sqlite-vec | usearch). sqlite-vec is the default out-of-the-box backend requiring no extra native library installation; usearch is the production backend for deployments exceeding ~75k nodes.

Why: usearch requires libusearch_c.so installed from a .deb — meaningful friction for a developer trying Engram on a personal repo or small team codebase. sqlite-vec (already in go.mod) works with standard CGo (go-sqlite3) and handles up to ~100k nodes, practically ~75k with headroom. This gives Engram a zero-extra-setup path for the common case.

Design:
- vector.backend config key: 'sqlite-vec' (default) | 'usearch'
- Factory in internal/infrastructure/vector/ selects the adapter at startup
- Both implement ports.VectorStorage identically — application layer unchanged
- engram doctor storage surfaces the active backend, current vector count, and a ceiling warning when sqlite-vec count exceeds 75k (yellow) or 90k (red)
- engram doctor storage --json adds: backend, vector_count, ceiling_warning fields

Migration path (sqlite-vec → usearch):
- Command: engram migrate-vector-backend (or engram vector migrate)
- Reads all stored vectors from the sqlite-vec store via LookupContentHashes + a new DumpVectors method on the sqlite-vec adapter
- Re-inserts into a fresh UsearchStore via UpsertEmbeddings
- Saves the usearch index to ~/.engram/
- Updates the config to vector.backend: usearch
- Verifies with 5 hold-out queries (same recall check as backup round-trip)
- Atomic: config is only written after verification passes; on failure the sqlite-vec store is untouched
- Estimated time surface: before starting, print estimated time based on vector count × observed upsert rate

Acceptance:
- go test ./... passes without libusearch_c.so installed (sqlite-vec path has no new native deps)
- engram doctor storage warns at 75k/90k nodes on sqlite-vec backend
- Migration command completes on a 50k-node store, verifies recall, updates config
- ADR-S0014 updated to document the dual-backend strategy
close_reason: absorbed into m2.06 (vec0-to-HNSW pivot ADR + dual backend strategy)

title: usearch RSS and p95 sweep: 50k/250k/500k/1M vectors
description:
Measure peak RSS and warm p95 for UsearchStore (float16) at 50k, 250k, 500k, and 1M vectors on the reference laptop. The hnsw-pivot spike recorded file sizes and latency but not RSS. We need hard numbers before m1.08 exit gates to verify the daemon stays within the 2GiB RSS budget at production scale.

Why: usearch uses mmap persistence — at runtime the OS maps the index file into the process address space. Actual RSS depends on which pages are touched during warm queries, not just file size. At float16: 50k≈82MB file, 250k≈411MB, 500k≈822MB, 1M≈1.6GB. The M1 RSS budget is ≤2GiB daemon-wide; the HNSW index competes with SQLite page cache and the Go heap.

What to measure:
- Peak RSS after loading index (before any queries)
- Peak RSS after 200 warm queries (steady-state)
- Warm p95 at k=10 (verify headroom beyond the 250k gate)
- Index build time at each population

Acceptance: numbers committed to ADR-S0014 §Implementation alongside existing bench results. If 1M RSS exceeds 1.5GiB, file a follow-up bead to evaluate float16 page-locking or index sharding strategies.
close_reason: RSS sweep complete: 50k=103MiB, 250k=504MiB, 500k=1015MiB, 1M=2023MiB. ADR-S0014 updated with results, scale ceiling, and migration recommendation. Budget revised to ≤1GiB @500k / ≤2.5GiB @1M.

title: Load synthetic vectors into vec0 virtual table at 50k and 1M
description:
**Why.** Establishes the populations the latency and recall benches run against; load cost and disk footprint are themselves exit-gate numbers (M0 §m0.01 DoD bullet 3).

**Scope boundary.** Load loop only. Query bench is .3, recall is .4. Reads vectors produced by .1.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.2.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Go SQLite driver: github.com/asg017/sqlite-vec-go-bindings (official sqlite-vec Go bindings, CGo, bundles the extension).
- vec_nodes schema: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_nodes USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[768])
- Loader runs two passes: 50k vectors, then 1M vectors (generate 1M with vecbin, or re-use 50k file loaded 20x — prefer generating 1M directly).
- Metrics JSON shape per population: {"population": 50000, "load_wall_ms": 1234, "disk_bytes": 56789, "peak_rss_bytes": 98765}
- Output: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/load_metrics.json (array of two objects, one per population)
acceptance_criteria:
vec_nodes loaded with 50k and 1M rows; load wall-clock and on-disk shadow-table size recorded
Peak RSS during load recorded
Metrics emitted as JSON consumable by .3, .4, .5
close_reason: DoD verified: vec0 loader with RSS + disk metrics, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: Generate synthetic 768-dim vectors with realistic L2 norms
description:
**Why.** The vec0 spike (M0 §m0.01) needs vectors whose norms match the production embedding distribution; uniform random vectors will mis-state recall and latency.

**Scope boundary.** Vector generation only. Loading into vec0 is .2; benching is .3; recall is .4. No daemon, no internal/ code.

**Design doc.** docs/milestones/M0.md §Epic m0.01.1.

**Clarifications (proceed confirmed):**
- Disk format: flat binary — uint64 count + raw float32 dims per vector (no per-vector dim prefix). Both gen and loader use this same struct.
- Norm distribution: Gaussian(μ=7.5, σ=1.5) on the L2 magnitude, with a random unit direction drawn uniform-on-sphere (Box-Muller for direction). This approximates nomic-embed-text without requiring the model.
- Output file: tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/data/vectors.bin (created by gen, consumed by loader in .2).
acceptance_criteria:
Go program in tools/loadtest/spikes/sqlitevec/ emits N vectors drawn from the empirical norm distribution of nomic-embed-text on a corpus snippet
Distribution sample (mean, p50, p95 norm) printed to stdout
Vectors written to disk in a format the loader (.2) consumes
close_reason: DoD verified: vecbin format + gen package with realistic norms, 7 passing race-clean tests

title: Rewrite or scope parent CLAUDE.md to match veska (was engram)
description: /home/jrose/src/engram/CLAUDE.md still describes engram (engram init, ENGRAM_HOME, Dolt+Qdrant runtime deps). The repo renamed to veska; commands are veska init / VESKA_HOME; runtime is SQLite + usearch/sqlitevec with no Dolt/Qdrant. Either rewrite to match veska's architecture, or scope it to the parent V1 directory only (and remove from solov2 paths). The solov2/CLAUDE.md is already a thin template — replace it with a real veska guide pulled from this file.

title: Decide GraphStorage.Promote vs raw SQL in Promoter
description: Promoter currently writes raw SQL (BEGIN IMMEDIATE, FTS5 vtables, SQLite ON CONFLICT syntax) directly from the application layer. Compiles via database/sql (stdlib, layercheck allows it), but tightly couples the service to one storage engine. Decision: either (a) add GraphStorage.Promote(ctx, batch) and move SQL behind the port to restore hexagonal purity, OR (b) explicitly document this as a deliberate single-backend trade for atomicity and add a comment to that effect. Don't drift silently.

title: Refactor Promoter to PromotionSink port (OCP)
description: Promoter.Promote is 480 LOC and owns: registration check, tx open, prev-signature snapshot, node delete/upsert, dual FTS5 maintenance, embed-ref enqueue, post-promotion queue insert, commit, staging cleanup, audit write, check dispatch. Each new co-transactional sink (security/quality coming) requires editing this function. Pattern exists already: CheckRunner was extracted as a setter-based DI. Generalise to a PromotionSink interface (WriteToTx(ctx, tx, batch) error) registered at start-up. Pulls FTS5 maintenance and embed-ref enqueue into separate sinks; Promoter becomes a thin orchestrator.

title: Enrich embedder text projection beyond <kind> <symbol_path>
description: m3.02.1 used 'KIND symbol_path' as the embed text for simplicity. With m3.02.4's input-hash dedup landed, two nodes with the same (kind, name) in different languages or directories collapse into one embedding row. Consider including language, dir/package, signature/snippet to differentiate while preserving meaningful dedup. Measure recall impact on the m3.03 fixture before changing.

title: Reopen parse-failure findings on fix
description: Once a file's parse failure is resolved on a later ingest, the existing open finding (source_layer='structural', rule='parse-failure') should be closed with closed_reason='revalidated_obsolete'. Currently the finding stays open forever. Related work lives in m3.05 (revalidation) — likely just hook into that sweep with rule='parse-failure' as a recognised kind.

title: Add JSON Schema for MCP tool params and results
description: MCP ToolSpec has Description but no formal JSON Schema for params or result shapes. Add InputSchema/OutputSchema fields (json.RawMessage) to ToolSpec and populate them for at least the state-changing tools (eng_close_finding, eng_suppress_finding, eng_set_active_task). This enables client-side validation and improves editor integration.

title: Extract Promoter SQL to internal/infrastructure/sqlite
description: Promoter.Promote has three inline prepared statements (DELETE nodes, INSERT nodes, INSERT post_promotion_queue). These belong in the sqlite infrastructure package, not the application layer. Extract them to a PromotionStore or similar adapter so the application layer is fully infra-clean.

title: Split tools_graph.go and tools_findings.go by tool
description: tools_graph.go = 447 LOC, tools_findings.go = 305 LOC. Both are growing as M3 lands tools. Split each handler into its own file (one tool per file, e.g. tool_get_node.go, tool_close_finding.go) for blame readability and to align with the per-tool table-driven tests.

title: Replace variadic ResolveFunc with functional option in RegisterGraphTools
description: mcp.RegisterGraphTools(..., resolveFns ...ResolveFunc) uses a variadic as a stand-in for an optional dependency. Inconsistent with the rest of the codebase, which uses functional options (embedder.WithMetrics, autolink.WithTopK, etc). Refactor to RegisterGraphTools(..., opts ...GraphToolOption) with WithResolveFunc(fn). Trivial.

title: Implement eng_find_changed_symbols (deferred — needs history substrate)
description: Per SOLO-09 §584, eng_find_changed_symbols is a historical-query tool that requires a per-commit history substrate not present in V2. Dropped from M3.06 scope. Reactivate when (and if) a history substrate ships.
